audits.name: youtube-transcript
findings: Excellent specificity — concrete triggers (YouTube links, exact Czech phrases)
Clear negative: non-YouTube sources, embed-only use cases
Description slightly over 200 chars but justified by multilingual triggers
Czech trigger phrases ('stáhni přepis', 'co říká to video') are a strong differentiator
recommendations: Description is 277 chars — consider trimming slightly for token efficiency, but content quality outweighs length concern

audits.name: autoloop
findings: Strong trigger words: 'optimize this', 'iterate on', 'improve until', 'make this better'
Negative trigger correctly excludes one-time edits and no-metric cases
Overlap risk with skill-generator when user says 'improve skill X' — both could trigger
Karpathy Loop reference is a useful differentiator for technical users
recommendations: Add 'Do NOT use when /skill-generator was already invoked for the same target' or clarify the distinction from skill-generator in description
Consider: 'iterative loop with measurable metric' vs skill-generator 'create/update skill'

audits.name: budget
findings: Good: 'Auto-invoked by orchestrator' signals system-level integration
Good: specific negative (simple cost questions) prevents frivolous invocation
Trigger conditions lean on orchestration context — less useful for standalone invocation
Missing explicit user-facing triggers (what does user type to invoke this?)
recommendations: Add explicit user trigger phrase: 'check budget', 'how much have we spent', 'set budget limit'
Current description is orchestration-context-heavy; add one user-facing phrase for discoverability

audits.name: checkpoint
findings: Excellent multilingual triggers: English + Czech ('ulož stav')
Very specific negative: 'mid-task when work is incomplete'
Clear use cases: ending session, large context, explicit user request
Description is 299 chars — longest in set, but content justifies it
recommendations: Could trim slightly: 'or when the user says' is implicit from trigger list
Consider: 299 chars is above 200 target but quality is high — acceptable tradeoff

audits.name: dependency-audit
findings: Good: 'after /watch reports version gaps' creates explicit cross-skill integration
Good: negative correctly redirects to /incident-runbook for runtime errors
Overlap with /watch: both scan for outdated dependencies — distinction is /dependency-audit is project-specific, /watch is ecosystem-wide
Missing: trigger for 'before major refactor' or 'upgrade X' pattern
recommendations: Add trigger: 'before upgrading', 'security audit', 'check if X is outdated'
Clarify vs /watch: 'for project-specific packages (not ecosystem news — use /watch)'

audits.name: incident-runbook
findings: Excellent: rich multilingual trigger list including Czech ('nefunguje', 'padá to', 'chyba')
Very clear negative: planned changes, feature requests, performance optimization
No overlap risk — 'unexpected failures' is unambiguous domain
Trigger word density is high — Claude will reliably recognize incident context
recommendations: No significant changes needed — best-in-class description for trigger reliability

audits.name: orchestrate
findings: Strong: '3+ files' is a concrete, unambiguous threshold
Good: explicitly redirects to /harness for repeatable processes
Overlap with /harness: the boundary ('known repeatable' vs 'unknown scope') is in description but subtle
Overlap with /scout: 'before implementing' could trigger either scout or orchestrate
Description at 311 chars is longest — justified by the skill's centrality
recommendations: The harness/orchestrate distinction is good but could be sharper: add 'if you've done this task before, use /harness'
Consider adding 'Do NOT use for exploring only (use /scout)' to prevent scout confusion

audits.name: project-init
findings: Very specific use case: brand new project setup
Negative is precise: 'existing projects with .claude/ already set up'
No overlap — unique niche, well-bounded
Length is good: 222 chars, information-dense
recommendations: Could add trigger: 'start new project', 'init project', 'set up claude config'

audits.name: scout
findings: Good: 'what do we have for X', 'map this area', 'scope this' are memorable trigger phrases
Good: 'Not for implementing changes' is a clear functional boundary
Overlap with orchestrate when user wants to 'understand before changing' — both could fire
The 'before any multi-file change' trigger could cause both scout AND orchestrate to activate simultaneously
Missing: explicit 'read-only / explore only' language in description
recommendations: Add: 'read-only exploration only — for multi-step changes after scouting, use /orchestrate'
Emphasize the sequential relationship: scout → then orchestrate

audits.name: scribe
findings: Good: 'auto-invoked by orchestrator' establishes system role
Good: specific negative ('ephemeral task notes')
Triggers are generic ('record this', 'remember that') — could match general conversation
Missing: stronger differentiation from general 'save to memory' intent
recommendations: Add: 'for structured memory in .claude/memory/ files (state.md, decisions.md, learnings.md)'
More specific trigger: 'record to shared memory', 'log this decision', 'update state.md'

audits.name: skill-generator
findings: Excellent: concrete trigger phrases ('create a skill', 'new slash command', 'improve skill X')
Good: negative explicitly excludes harnesses and one-off scripts
Overlap with autoloop: 'improve skill X' triggers both — autoloop does iterative optimization, skill-generator does one-shot improvement
Distinction from autoloop is implicit (iterative vs one-shot) — could be clearer
recommendations: Add: 'for iterative automated optimization of a skill with scoring, use /autoloop instead'
The 'update skill' trigger could be more specific: 'update skill structure/frontmatter'

audits.name: verify
findings: Strong: 'prove it', 'does it actually work' are high-intent triggers
Good Czech triggers: 'dokaž to', 'funguje to?'
Good: negative explicitly excludes unit tests and syntax checks
Overlap with critic: both 'review' and 'verify' can sound similar, but verify is execution-based, critic is analysis-based
The distinction ('end-to-end proof on real data') is clear
recommendations: Consider adding: 'runs real code — not analysis (use /critic for analysis-only review)'
Current description is strong — minor clarification only

audits.name: watch
findings: Good: time-based trigger ('last scan >7 days old') is unique and concrete
Good: negative correctly redirects project-specific to /dependency-audit
Trigger 'what's new' is very generic — could fire in unrelated contexts
Missing: explicit mention of ecosystem scope (Anthropic, AI/ML, not general web)
The 'check for updates' trigger is ambiguous (updates to what?)
recommendations: More specific triggers: 'what's new in Claude Code', 'any AI news', 'scan ecosystem updates'
Add: 'for AI/ML and Claude Code ecosystem only — not general web research'

audits.name: critic
findings: Excellent: 'auto-invoked after code edits' establishes proactive role
Good: 'budget critic iterations exhausted' is a sophisticated negative trigger
Good: clear separation from verify (analysis vs execution)
Overlap with verify is manageable — critic reviews, verify proves
Description well-calibrated for both auto-invocation and explicit use
recommendations: No significant issues — well-crafted description
Minor: could add 'Do NOT use when /verify was just run (avoid double-checking same evidence)'

audits.name: harness
findings: Strong: '/harness' as explicit trigger is reliable
Good: 'known fixed steps' vs '/orchestrate' unknown scope is well-articulated
The orchestrate/harness distinction is the right boundary — matches design intent
Czech trigger 'spusť harness' matches user's language preference
The 'structured output' mention adds useful differentiation
recommendations: No major issues
Consider: add 'repeatable, auditable process with phase-level validation' to strengthen the structured-output angle
